news.title: SBI agrees to alternate plots as Raidurg land dispute ends
news.para: The Telangana government and the State Bank of India have resolved a land dispute. SBI's board approved an offer for alternate land parcels in Hyderabad. This agreement will likely lead to the withdrawal of a pending high court case. The bank will receive land in Knowledge City and Bharat Future City. This resolution ends a significant dispute over valuable Hyderabad real estate.

news.title: LDA to conduct fire safety audit of eight lakh buildings in Lucknow
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority will audit eight lakh buildings for fire safety. A private agency will conduct inspections of electrical wiring and building access. Buildings will receive a safety rating displayed on a public plate. This initiative follows a recent fire incident in Aliganj. Audit reports will aid the Fire Department in corrective actions.

news.title: Kolkata civic body lifts bar on G+4 construction, high-rise safety-critical work
news.para: Kolkata Municipal Corporation has eased construction restrictions for buildings up to five storeys. High-rise projects can now complete essential safety work without needing prior approval. This decision follows concerns raised by developers and engineers about potential hazards. Owners of low-rise buildings can also proceed with construction and repairs. The revised notification aims to ensure site safety and prevent structural issues.

news.title: One lakh patta holders hit; Ramsar freeze has stalled South Chennai growth: CREDAI
news.para: CREDAI states over one lakh patta holders face a development crisis in South Chennai. Construction worth Rs 1 lakh crore is stalled, impacting government revenue significantly. Developers urge CMDA to review the one-kilometer Ramsar influence zone demarcation. This freeze affects Velachery, Pallikaranai, and other key areas. The association seeks ground-truthing and proper surveys for boundary finalization.

news.title: Stamp duty for lease agreements of housing societies in Mumbai reduced to 0.5%
news.para: Maharashtra has significantly reduced stamp duty for cooperative housing societies on government land. Residential properties will now face a maximum duty of 0.5 percent. Commercial properties will be subject to a 1.5 percent stamp duty rate. This policy change offers substantial financial relief to many housing societies. The government also waived transfer premiums for certain flat owners.

news.title: Delhi: CIC tells MCD to publish residential lift plans, maps, approvals in wider public interest
news.para: The Central Information Commission advised the Municipal Corporation of Delhi. It urged proactive public disclosure of residential lift installation plans and related records. This move aims to enhance transparency and reduce individual Right to Information applications. The commission accepted the public information officer's explanation for a past delay. No penal action was deemed necessary for the officer's actions.

news.title: Ladakh LG approves interim land-use norms, eases rules for small plots within municipal limits
news.para: Ladakh's Lieutenant Governor approved an interim land-use framework for development.  This framework allows certain land uses without prior approval within municipal limits.  It aims to remove uncertainty and ease building permissions for citizens.  Prohibited activities are clearly defined to ensure proper regulation and safeguards.  This interim measure will remain until statutory planning instruments are officially notified.

news.title: eClinicalWorks founder's family buys Worli luxury apartment at ₹2.5 lakh per sq ft
news.para: Radhi Navani bought a luxury sea-facing apartment in Mumbai for Rs 162.2 crore. This transaction marks one of the country's costliest residential property deals. The apartment is located on the 18th floor of Naman Xana in Worli. The buyer benefited from a stamp duty concession for women homebuyers. This acquisition highlights continued demand for high-end homes in Mumbai.

news.title: Haryana RERA sets aside Neo Developers’ unit cancellation notice
news.para: Haryana RERA’s Gurugram bench has directed Neo Developers to pay assured returns of ₹1.08 lakh per month to the allottee from October 10, 2020 till a valid offer of possession. The authority also directed the developer to hand over possession within 60 days and execute the conveyance deed within three months.

news.title: Delhi-NCR flex space accounts for 45% of 3.6 million sq ft office leasing in Q2 2026: CBRE
news.para: Flexible space operators accounted for 45% of Delhi-NCR’s total office leasing of about 3.6 million sq ft in Q2 2026, according to CBRE. Flex leasing in the region stood at about 1.6 million sq ft during the quarter.

news.title: Chandigarh estate office doubles revenue recovery in FY26
news.para: The UT estate office significantly increased revenue recovery in 2025-26. Collections rose to Rs 335.05 crore, more than doubling the previous year's amount. This achievement resulted from stricter enforcement and digital reforms implemented by the office. Focused recovery drives and faster disposal of pending matters also contributed. Future plans include continued drives and e-auctions to sustain revenue.

news.title: Public consultation on Dehradun master plan 2041 begins
news.para: A 12-day public consultation for Dehradun Master Plan 2041 commenced Monday. Residents submitted eighteen suggestions and objections during the first sector hearing. Hearings for remaining sectors will continue until July twenty-first. The plan addresses population growth and infrastructure needs for the city. Public feedback will help finalize the master plan's practical implementation.

news.title: CAQM finds 62 violations of pollution norms across NCR industries, C&D sites from Jun 14-30
news.para: Inspections across the national capital region found sixty-two violations of pollution control norms. These violations occurred at construction, demolition, and industrial sites during June. Flying squads conducted numerous inspections across various categories of sites. Actions were taken on all actionable cases identified by the enforcement task force. Seven resumption orders were issued after compliance verification in Uttar Pradesh and Haryana.

news.title: Housing minister distributes sanction letters to 31 PMAY-Urban beneficiaries
news.para: Union Minister Manohar Lal Khattar distributed sanction letters to thirty-one beneficiaries.  He reaffirmed the Centre's commitment to providing permanent housing for eligible poor families.  The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban 2.0 aims to house economically weaker urban families.  Haryana and the Centre are jointly implementing welfare initiatives for poor households.  Two thousand one hundred seventy-four beneficiaries across Haryana have been approved.

news.title: Gurugram: Notices served to illegal buildings in Sultanpur National park buffer
news.para: Authorities began enforcement actions within Sultanpur National Park's eco-sensitive zone on Monday. Joint teams inspected properties to verify permissions and construction status for alleged violations. The forest department issued initial notices, with under-construction buildings slated for demolition first. Many property owners failed to produce mandatory land use approvals and other required documents. Further action, including demolitions, will proceed in phases after a meeting with the deputy commissioner.

news.title: South India-based developers step up Mumbai play amid strong housing demand
news.para: Several large South Indian real estate developers are now entering Mumbai's property market. They are strategically focusing on redevelopment projects and joint development agreements. This approach offers a capital-efficient entry into India's most expensive housing market. Mumbai's sustained housing demand and redevelopment opportunities attract these new entrants. The trend is intensifying competition in the city's premium and mid-income housing segments.

news.title: NBW issued against Deluris Buildtech India's MD, director in compensation case
news.para: Non-bailable warrants were issued against Deluris Buildtech directors for failing to comply with commission orders. Homebuyers alleged delayed possession of apartments in the Zara Rossa project. The commission awarded compensation which the developer allegedly did not fulfill. Deluris Buildtech stated possession was handed over after receiving the occupancy certificate. The company also mentioned pending review petitions and appeals regarding compensation.

news.title: Nestlé Business Solutions to set up GCC in Hyderabad
news.para: Nestlé Business Solutions will set up a global capability centre in Hyderabad under Nestlé Business Services India. The centre will be developed in partnership with Genpact and will focus on data, AI, automation and digital operations.
